task_completion.description: Experiment is saved to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PsychoPy window title or save dialog shows the file name 'motion_coherence.psyexp' and the path '~/PsychoPyExperiments/' or '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/'.

task_completion.description: The experiment flow contains the required routines in order
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel at the bottom of the PsychoPy Builder shows the routines 'instructions', 'fixation', 'trial', and 'thanks' in sequential order.

task_completion.description: Instructions, fixation, and thanks routines contain the correct components and durations
task_completion.visual_evidence: The routine tabs show Text components in 'instructions', 'fixation', and 'thanks', plus a Keyboard component in 'instructions'. Properties windows (if visible) show 0.5s duration for the fixation cross and 2.0s duration for the thanks message.

task_completion.description: A loop named 'trials' encompasses the fixation and trial routines with the correct conditions file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flow panel shows a loop named 'trials' wrapping around the 'fixation' and 'trial' routines. The loop properties dialog shows the conditions file set to 'conditions/motion_conditions.csv' and 'nReps' set to 2.

task_completion.description: The RDK (Dots) component is configured with the correct parameters
task_completion.visual_evidence: The properties dialog for the Dots component in the 'trial' routine shows 'nDots' set to 200, 'fieldShape' set to 'circle', 'dotLife' set to 3, 'coherence' set to '$coherence', and 'direction' set to '$direction'.

task_completion.description: The Keyboard component in the trial routine is configured with correct allowed keys and answer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The properties dialog for the Keyboard component in the 'trial' routine shows allowed keys as 'left', 'right' (or similar list format) and the correct answer field set to '$corrAns'.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to create the experiment
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the PsychoPy Builder interface (adding components, editing properties, managing flow) rather than writing a Python script or editing the .psyexp XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used the required variables instead of hardcoding values
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent entered the variables '$coherence', '$direction', and '$corrAns' into the component properties, rather than hardcoding specific numerical values or directions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The experiment must be saved to /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/motion_coherence.psyexp. The conditions file is located at /home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/conditions/motion_conditions.csv and contains variables: coherence, direction, corrAns. The experiment requires Dots, Keyboard, and Text components.
